2603.19028.title: SEM: Sparse Embedding Modulation for Post-Hoc Debiasing of Vision-Language Models
2603.19028.paper_summary: Models that bridge vision and language, such as CLIP, are key components of multimodal AI, yet their large-scale, uncurated training data introduce severe social and spurious biases. Existing post-hoc debiasing methods often operate directly in the dense CLIP embedding space, where bias and task-relevant information are highly entangled. This entanglement limits their ability to remove bias without degrading semantic fidelity. In this work, we propose Sparse Embedding Modulation (SEM), a post-hoc, zero-shot debiasing framework that operates in a Sparse Autoencoder (SAE) latent space. By decomposing CLIP text embeddings into disentangled features, SEM identifies and modulates bias-relevant neurons while preserving query-relevant ones. This enables more precise, non-linear interventions. Across four benchmark datasets and two CLIP backbones, SEM achieves substantial fairness gains in retrieval and zero-shot classification. Our results demonstrate that sparse latent representations provide an effective foundation for post-hoc debiasing of vision-language models.
2603.19028.paper_summary_zh: 橋接視覺與語言的模型，如CLIP，是多模態AI的關鍵組成部分，但其大規模、未經篩選的訓練數據引入了嚴重的社會和虛假偏見。現有的事後去偏見方法通常直接在密集的CLIP嵌入空間中運作，偏見與任務相關信息高度交織。這種交織限制了它們在不降低語義真實性的情況下去除偏見的能力。在這項工作中，我們提出了稀疏嵌入調制（SEM），這是一種在稀疏自編碼器（SAE）潛在空間中運作的事後零樣本去偏見框架。通過將CLIP文本嵌入分解為解耦特徵，SEM識別並調制與偏見相關的神經元，同時保留與查詢相關的神經元。這使得更精確的非線性干預成為可能。在四個基準數據集和兩個CLIP骨幹上，SEM在檢索和零樣本分類中實現了顯著的公平性提升。我們的結果表明，稀疏潛在表示為視覺-語言模型的事後去偏見提供了有效的基礎。
2603.19028.author: Quentin Guimard et.al.
2603.19028.authors: Quentin Guimard, Federico Bartsch, Simone Caldarella, Rahaf Aljundi, Elisa Ricci, Massimiliano Mancini
